---
title: "BEAR"
subtitle: "Benchmarks of Empirical Accuracy in Research"
---

![](assets/bear_banner.png){.bear-banner fig-alt="BEAR banner"}

**Problem**: Quantitative metascientific deals with important issues such as 
publication bias, p-hacking, reporting practices, pre-registration, significance rates, and more. A lot of crucial contributions of metascience is based on analysing 
individual datasets, often painstakingly constructed by researchers. This means that there is a wealth of data we can use to test metascientific claims and methodologies.


**Solution**: BEAR is a open-source database for metascience exploration
with 25 datasets across many scientific fields and millions of data points. 
BEAR does not generate any new source data; it repackages public datasets 
into a common structure for research on replication, exchangeability, 
meta-analysis, and related questions about empirical accuracy.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
To download latest version of data, head through
[GitHub Releases](https://github.com/wwiecek/BEAR/releases). 
or you can follow [download instructions](getting-started.qmd) for command line.
:::

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
## What is included?

BEAR combines curated single-study datasets, collections of
meta-analyses, large text-mined biomedical datasets, clinical-trial
databases, and direct replication projects. Head to [datasets section](datasets.qmd)
to read about individual datasets.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
## Who is this for?

The project is intended for researchers who want a common empirical base
for studying statistical evidence across fields, publication bias,
replicability, power, exchangeability, and the behaviour of meta-analytic
datasets.
:::
:::

## Links

- [Get data](getting-started.qmd) for release downloads and a minimal R
  loading example.
- [Datasets](datasets.qmd) for the current dataset index and individual
  dataset notes.
- [Reproduce](reproduce.qmd) for the repository workflow and rebuild
  entry points.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/wwiecek/BEAR) for code, issues,
  and releases.
